groups.title: Advice on family visit
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Accommodation · $22075$ Hotel on George Street ·cost of family room per night: £$22076$ (approx.) Recommended trips · a $22077$ tour of the city centre (starts in Carlton Square) · a trip by $22078$ to the old fort Science Museum · best day to visit: $22079$ · see the exhibition about $22080$, which opens soon Food · Clacton Market: - good for $22081$ food - need to have lunch before $22082$ p.m. Theatre tickets · save up to $22083$ % on ticket prices at bargaintickets.com Free activities · Blakewell Gardens: - Roots Music Festival - climb Telegraph Hill to see a view of the $22084$

questions.analysis: 这道题需要填入酒店名称，题干中的定位词是“Hotel”和“George Street”。在听力中，男士提到“my cousin wants me to recommend a hotel”，这里的“hotel”与题干定位词“Hotel”对应，是寻找答案的信号。随后女士回答“I always recommend people stay at the Kings Hotel”，直接给出了酒店名称“Kings Hotel”。接着男士询问位置，女士补充“It’s actually on George Street”，其中“George Street”与题干中的定位词完全一致，进一步确认了前面提到的“Kings Hotel”就是位于乔治街的酒店。因此，答案“Kings”（或“King's”）是正确的，它是酒店名称的核心部分，且与题干中的位置信息完全匹配。

questions.answer: 125 / one hundred and twenty-five / one hundred twenty-five
questions.analysis: 这道题的定位词是“family room”，在听力原文中，桑德拉直接提到了“family room”这个词，这就是答案即将出现的信号。紧接着她明确说明“it’s about £125 per night”，这里的“per night”和题干中的“per night”完全对应，“about”也与题干括号里的“approx.”（大约）意思一致。虽然原文后面还提到了“two double rooms... around £95 for each room”，但“double rooms”（双人房）和题目问的“family room”（家庭房）是不同的房间类型，属于干扰信息。所以，根据定位词“family room”锁定的答案句，正确答案就是125。

这道题的答案是“walking”。首先，题干中的定位词“Carlton Square”和“city centre”能帮我们锁定答案区域。男士先提到想做“bus tour of the city centre”（市中心巴士游览），但女士Sandra否定了这个提议，说“Those bus tours are quite expensive”（巴士游览太贵了），并推荐“it’s better to do a walking tour”（最好做徒步游览）。接着她补充道“There’s one that starts from Carlton Square”（有一个徒步游览从卡尔顿广场开始），这里的“starts from Carlton Square”与题干括号里的“starts in Carlton Square”完全对应，说明女士推荐的“walking tour”就是题干所问的“从卡尔顿广场开始的市中心游览”。所以答案只能是“walking”。
题目问的是“去古堡的旅行方式是乘坐什么”，定位词是“old fort”。在听力原文中，当Sandra提到“visit the old fort”（参观古堡）时，紧接着就说明了“ You can get there by boat”（你可以乘船到那里）。这里的“by”明确提示了交通方式，后面的“boat”就是具体的交通工具，所以答案是“boat”。

这道题的定位词是“best day”和“visit”。“visit”对应对话中讨论的“Science Museum”（科学博物馆）这一参观地点；“best day”则直接对应原文中男士说的“Tuesday would be best”（周二是最好的）。对话中还提到周一博物馆“closed”（关闭），周六“terrible”（人太多，很糟糕），这两个时间都被排除了。所以综合来看，“Tuesday”（周二）就是参观的最佳日子，因此答案是Tuesday。

这道题需要找到“即将开放的展览主题”。首先，题干中的定位词“exhibition”在原文中通过“SANDRA: I took my kids to the exhibition on old computers there...”直接出现，但这里提到的“old computers”展览后紧跟着“it’s finished now”（现在已经结束了），与题干“opens soon”（即将开放）矛盾，所以“computers”是干扰项。接着，定位词“opens soon”的同义替换“starting soon”出现在“SANDRA: There’s another one starting soon on space...”中，“another one”指代“exhibition”，明确说明这是“另一个展览”，且“starting soon”对应“opens soon”，后面的“on space”直接表明展览主题是“space”，因此答案为“space”。

这道题的题干是“good for 【 】 food”，需要我们找到形容“food”的词。首先，题干中的定位词是“food”，在听力中，当Sandra问“Have you thought about where to take them to eat?”时，“eat”就是“food”的同义替换信号词，提示我们接下来要讨论与食物相关的内容。接着男士提到“food stalls at Clacton Market”，并说“My cousin’s vegetarian – I know it’s one of the best places for that kind of food”，这里的“one of the best places for”和题干中的“good for”是同义替换，而“that kind of food”指代的就是前文提到的“vegetarian”（素食者）对应的食物类型。所以，“vegetarian”就是形容“food”的正确答案。

题目问的是“需要在下午几点前吃午饭”，核心定位词是“lunch”。在听力原文中，当讨论到“where to take them to eat”时，桑德拉提到“most of the stalls stop serving lunch at 2.30”，这里直接出现了定位词“lunch”。“停止供应午餐”的意思就是“必须在停止供应前吃完午餐”，所以“2.30”就是需要填入的时间，即必须在下午2点30分前吃午饭。

这道题的答案是75。首先，我们通过题干中的定位词“bargaintickets.com”（网站名称，专有名词，不易替换）在听力原文中定位到关键信息。当听到“bargaintickets.com”时，就要注意后面出现的与“save up to ...%”（节省百分比）相关的内容。原文中紧接着提到“on some seats there’s a 75% discount”，这里的“discount”（折扣）和题干中的“save”（节省）是同义替换，“75% discount”即表示“save up to 75%”，所以空格处应填入75。

这道题的定位词是“Telegraph Hill”，它是专有名词，在听力原文中直接出现，没有同义替换，听到这个词就说明答案即将出现。原文中提到“climb Telegraph Hill – you’ll be able to look right down on the port”，这里的“look right down on”（俯瞰）和题干中的“see a view of”（看到……的景色）是同义替换，所以“look right down on”后面的名词“port”就是答案，即爬上电报山是为了看港口的景色。

transcript.en: PART 1
MAN: Sandra – I seem to remember you had some family visitors staying with you recently.
transcript.cn: 男人：桑德拉 —— 我好像记得最近有一些家庭访客住在你那里。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Yeah that's right. My brother and his family were here a couple of months ago.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：是的，没错。我哥哥和他的家人几个月前来过这里。

transcript.en: MAN: OK good. Well I wanted to ask your advice – I've got my cousin and her family visiting next month and as I don't have kids I've no idea where to take them.
transcript.cn: 男人：好的，很好。我想问问你的建议 —— 我表妹和她的家人下个月要来拜访，我没有孩子，不知道该带他们去哪里。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Right. What about accommodation? Are they going to stay with you in your flat?
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：好的。住处怎么办？他们会和你一起住在你的公寓里吗？

transcript.en: MAN: No, thankfully – there wouldn't be room – my cousin wants me to recommend a hotel. Do you know anywhere?
transcript.cn: 男人：不，谢天谢地 —— 没有地方了 —— 我表哥想让我推荐一家酒店。你知道哪里有这样的地方吗？

transcript.en: SANDRA: Yes, I do actually. I always recommend people stay at the Kings Hotel.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：是的，其实我知道。我总是推荐人们住在国王酒店。

transcript.en: MAN: Where's that near?
transcript.cn: 男人：那离这儿近吗？

transcript.en: SANDRA: It's about five minutes' walk from Murray Station – so nice and central. It's actually on George Street.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：从默里车站步行大约五分钟就到了 —— 那里很漂亮，而且在市中心。实际上，它就在乔治街上。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh yes I know. I think they're on quite a tight budget – so how much roughly is it to stay there?
transcript.cn: 男人：哦，是的，我知道。我想他们的预算相当紧张 —— 那么住在那里大概需要多少钱？

transcript.en: SANDRA: If you book a family room it's about £125 per night.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：如果你预订客房，每晚大约 125 英镑。

transcript.en: My brother paid for two double rooms in the end and I think that was around £95 for each room.
transcript.cn: 我哥哥最后付了两间双人房的钱，我记得每间房大约 95 英镑。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh that's not too bad.
transcript.cn: 男人：哦，那还不错。

transcript.en: SANDRA: So how old are your cousin's kids?
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：那么，你表哥的孩子们多大了？

transcript.en: MAN: Twelve and nine. So I want to organise some trips while they're here.
transcript.cn: 男人：十二岁和九岁。所以我想趁他们在这里的时候组织一些旅行。

transcript.en: I was thinking of doing a bus tour of the city centre – as none of them have been here before.
transcript.cn: 我正在考虑乘坐巴士游览市中心 —— 因为他们以前都没来过这里。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Those bus tours are quite expensive. I think it's better to do a walking tour – it gives you a much better feel for the city.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：那些巴士游相当昂贵。我觉得步行游会更好 —— 这会让你对这座城市有更好的感受。

transcript.en: There's one that starts from Carlton Square. It takes a couple of hours and doesn't cost that much.
transcript.cn: 有一条从卡尔顿广场开始的路线。它只需要几个小时，而且并不贵。

transcript.en: MAN: Sounds good. I'll look that up – thanks.
transcript.cn: 男人：听起来不错。我去查查 —— 谢谢。

transcript.en: SANDRA: If the weather's nice, one thing you could do is visit the old fort.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：如果天气好的话，你可以去参观一下那座古老的堡垒。

transcript.en: You can get there by boat. The whole trip takes half a day.
transcript.cn: 你可以坐船去，整个旅程只需要半天时间。

transcript.en: MAN: That's a really good idea. I'd like to do that myself.
transcript.cn: 男人：这真是个好主意。我想自己去。

transcript.en: And if the weather's bad I was thinking they could go to the Science Museum. But maybe they could do that when I'm at work.
transcript.cn: 如果天气不好，我想他们可以去科学博物馆。不过也许他们可以在我工作的时候去。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Yeah – don't forget it's closed on Mondays.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：是啊 —— 别忘了周一博物馆是关门的。

transcript.en: MAN: They're here from Saturday for four nights – so Tuesday would be best, I think.
transcript.cn: 男人：他们从周六开始在这里待了四个晚上 —— 所以我觉得周二会是最好的。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Mmm, and it won't be so crowded then. Saturdays are terrible.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：嗯，到时候就不会那么拥挤了。星期六真是太糟糕了。

transcript.en: I took my kids to the exhibition on old computers there and it was far too crowded.
transcript.cn: 我带孩子们去看了那里的旧电脑展览，那里实在是太拥挤了。

transcript.en: I wanted to go back but it's ffnished now.
transcript.cn: 我本想回去的，但现在已经结束了。

transcript.en: MAN: That's a shame. My cousin's kids would have enjoyed that.
transcript.cn: 男人：真可惜。我表妹的孩子们会喜欢那个的。

transcript.en: SANDRA: There's another one starting soon on space which looks really good too.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：太空中还有一个很快就要开始的项目，看起来也很不错。

transcript.en: MAN: OK, well I'll mention that to my cousin.
transcript.cn: 男人：好吧，我会跟我表哥提一下的。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Have you thought about where to take them to eat?
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：你想过带他们去哪里吃饭吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Well I really like all the food stalls at Clacton Market. My cousin's vegetarian – I know it's one of the best places for that kind of food.
transcript.cn: 男人：嗯，我真的很喜欢克拉克顿市场的所有食品摊位。我表哥是素食主义者 —— 我知道那是最适合吃素食的地方之一。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Definitely, and there'll be loads of choice for the kids, too.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：当然，孩子们也有很多选择。

transcript.en: You need to get there quite early though – at the weekend most of the stalls stop serving lunch at 2.30.
transcript.cn: 不过你得早点到那里 —— 周末的时候，大多数摊位两点半就停止供应午餐了。

transcript.en: MAN: Good point. It's all going to need careful planning!
transcript.cn: 男人：说得好。这一切都需要仔细规划！

transcript.en: My cousin said she'd love to take the kids to a show at the theatre – but tickets are so expensive!
transcript.cn: 我表姐说她很想带孩子们去剧院看演出 —— 但是票太贵了！

transcript.en: SANDRA: I know. But you can get some good deals if you book online with bargaintickets.com for the following day – on some seats there's a 75% discount.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：我知道。但是如果你在 baganticketets.com 网站上预订第二天的票，就能得到一些不错的折扣 —— 有些座位还有 75% 的折扣。

transcript.en: MAN: Really?! I must try and get some.
transcript.cn: 男人：真的吗？！我一定要试试看能不能弄到一些。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Yeah. There are lots of things you can do for free as well – no need to spend a fortune.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：是的。你也可以免费做很多事情 —— 不需要花一大笔钱。

transcript.en: MAN: Like what?
transcript.cn: 男人：比如说？

transcript.en: SANDRA: They're coming next month, right?
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：他们下个月会来，对吧？

transcript.en: Well, check and see if it's the same weekend as the Roots Music Festival in Blakewell Gardens.
transcript.cn: 好吧，那就查查看这个周末是不是和布莱克韦尔花园的鲁茨音乐节在同一个周末。

transcript.en: MAN: R double-O-T-S?
transcript.cn: 男人：R 双 O-T-S?

transcript.en: SANDRA: Yeah. Check it out online. It's always a family-friendly event and there's no entry charge.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：是的。你可以在网上查看。这始终是一个家庭友好型活动，而且不收取入场费。

transcript.en: MAN: That sounds perfect.
transcript.cn: 男人：听起来很完美。

transcript.en: SANDRA: And if you're in Blakewell Gardens – climb Telegraph Hill – you'll be able to look right down on the port.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：如果你在布莱克韦尔花园 —— 爬上电报山 —— 你就能直接俯瞰港口。

transcript.en: Everyone's always really impressed because it's so huge.
transcript.cn: 每个人都对它印象深刻，因为它实在太大了。

transcript.en: MAN: Oh yeah – I've been meaning to do that for ages. I've heard the view's amazing.
transcript.cn: 男人：哦，是啊 —— 我早就想这么做了。我听说那里的景色很棒。

transcript.en: SANDRA: Yeah. It's really worth the effort.
transcript.cn: 桑德拉：是的，这真的很值得。

transcript.en: MAN: Well that's given me loads of ideas. Thanks so much …
transcript.cn: 男人：嗯，这给了我很多想法。非常感谢......

groups.title: Which TWO things does the speaker say about visiting the football stadium with children?
groups.desc:
Questions 11 and 12
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: Children can get their photo taken with a football player.

list.title: B
list.content: There is a competition for children today.

list.title: C
list.content: Parents must stay with their children at all times.

list.title: D
list.content: Children will need sunhats and drinks.

list.title: E
list.content: The café has a special offer on meals for children.

collect_option.title: Which TWO things does the speaker say about visiting the football stadium with children?

questions.analysis: 题目询问演讲者关于带孩子参观足球场提到的两件事。对于选项B，原文提到“children are invited to do a football-themed drawing”且“pick the best one”，即邀请孩子参与绘画并评选最佳作品，这属于“competition（比赛）”的同义转述；对于选项C，原文“don’t let your children wander off on their own, even for a minute”明确要求家长不让孩子独自走开，意味着“Parents must stay with their children at all times（家长必须随时和孩子待在一起）”，两者意思一致，因此B和C为正确答案。

题目询问演讲者关于带孩子参观足球场提到的两件事。对于选项B，原文提到“children are invited to do a football-themed drawing”且“pick the best one”，即邀请孩子参与绘画并评选最佳作品，这属于“competition（比赛）”的同义转述；对于选项C，原文“don’t let your children wander off on their own, even for a minute”明确要求家长不让孩子独自走开，意味着“Parents must stay with their children at all times（家长必须随时和孩子待在一起）”，两者意思一致，因此B和C为正确答案。

questions.analysis: 题目询问的是今年体育场参观的哪两个特色是新增的。在听力原文中，当提到“self-guided”参观方式并领取耳机收听预录信息时，明确指出“We’ve only just introduced this feature”（我们刚刚才推出了这项功能），这里的“only just introduced”是“new”的同义替换，对应选项C的“audio guide”。随后，在提到“VIP tours”时，又提到“we’ve only just started offering these”（我们也是刚刚才开始提供这项服务），“only just started offering”同样是“new”的同义替换，对应选项A的“VIP tour”。因此，这两项是今年新增的特色。

题目询问的是今年体育场参观的哪两个特色是新增的。在听力原文中，当提到“self-guided”参观方式并领取耳机收听预录信息时，明确指出“We’ve only just introduced this feature”（我们刚刚才推出了这项功能），这里的“only just introduced”是“new”的同义替换，对应选项C的“audio guide”。随后，在提到“VIP tours”时，又提到“we’ve only just started offering these”（我们也是刚刚才开始提供这项服务），“only just started offering”同样是“new”的同义替换，对应选项A的“VIP tour”。因此，这两项是今年新增的特色。

groups.title: Events in the history of football
groups.desc:
Questions 15 - 20
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A – H, next to Questions 15 – 20.
Which event in the history of football in the UK took place in each of the following years?

list.title: A
list.content: the introduction of pay for the players

list.title: B
list.content: a change to the design of the goal

list.title: C
list.content: the first use of lights for matches

list.title: F
list.content: two changes to the rules of the game

list.title: G
list.content: the introduction of a fee for spectators

list.title: H
list.content: an agreement on the length of a game

collect_option.title: Events in the history of football

questions.analysis: 题目询问1870年英国足球历史上发生的事件。根据听力原文，1870年明确提到“football teams started to include a player whose role it was to guard the goal”（足球队开始纳入一名职责为守卫球门的球员），“guard the goal”（守卫球门）的球员即“goalkeepers”（守门员），“started to include”（开始纳入）对应“introduction”（引入），因此该事件为“引入守门员”，对应选项D。

题目询问1874年英国足球历史上发生的事件。根据听力原文，1874年明确提到了两件事：一是“referees were allowed to send players off”（裁判被允许将犯规球员罚下场），二是“teams started having to swap ends at half-time”（球队开始在中场休息时交换场地）。这两件事均属于足球比赛规则的变更，因此对应选项F“two changes to the rules of the game”（两项比赛规则的变更）。

文中明确提到1875年发生的事件是“that tape was replaced with the solid crossbar”（带子被坚固的横梁替换）。结合前文“the aim was for the scorer to get the ball between two flag posts, and later between sticks joined at the top with a piece of tape”可知，早期球门顶部是带子，1875年替换为横梁，这属于球门设计的改变，因此对应选项B“a change to the design of the goal”。

题目询问1877年英国足球历史上发生的事件。根据听力原文，1877年“all the clubs decided to set a limit of ninety minutes for each match”（所有俱乐部决定为每场比赛设定90分钟的时长限制），这表明该年份确定了比赛的时长标准。选项H“an agreement on the length of a game”（关于一场比赛时长的协议）与此描述完全对应，因此答案为H。

题目要求匹配1878年英国足球史上发生的事件。根据听力原文，当提到年份“By 1878”时，明确指出“electric lamps were installed on certain pitches”（电灯被安装在一些球场上）。“electric lamps”（电灯）与选项C中的“lights”（灯光）为同义替换，安装电灯意味着比赛开始使用灯光，因此1878年对应的事件是“the first use of lights for matches”，答案为C。

题目要求匹配1880年英国足球历史上发生的事件。根据听力原文，1880年对应的关键信息是“clubs began to charge fans for admission to games”（俱乐部开始向球迷收取比赛门票费）。其中“charge fans for admission”（向球迷收入场费）与选项G“the introduction of a fee for spectators”（引入观众费用）直接对应，“fans”（球迷）和“spectators”（观众）为同义替换，“charge...for admission”（收取入场费）即“引入费用”，因此答案为G。

transcript.en: PART 2
Good morning, and welcome to City Football Club.
transcript.cn: 早上好，欢迎来到城市足球俱乐部。

transcript.en: I'd like to give you some useful information about your visit to the stadium today, and then we'll start the tour of the areas of the stadium that are open to visitors.
transcript.cn: 我想为大家提供一些关于今天体育场参观的有用信息，然后我们将开始参观体育场向游客开放的区域。

transcript.en: I can see lots of children here today, so just to let mums and dads know a few things before we start.
transcript.cn: 今天我看到这里有很多孩子，所以在我们开始之前，让爸爸妈妈们了解一些情况。

transcript.en: The stadium has lots of stairs, and the players' tunnel is very dark.
transcript.cn: 体育场有很多台阶，运动员通道非常昏暗。

transcript.en: Please don't let your children wander off on their own, even for a minute.
transcript.cn: 请不要让你的孩子独自走开，哪怕只有一分钟。

transcript.en: We don't want any accidents or anyone getting frightened.
transcript.cn: 我们不希望发生任何事故，也不希望任何人受到惊吓。

transcript.en: Cameras are permitted everywhere, and you can take pictures of your child shooting a penalty.
transcript.cn: 摄像机在任何地方都是允许的，你可以拍摄孩子罚点球的照片。

transcript.en: Assistants are helping to organise this, and hopefully the queue won't be too long – it's very hot and sunny out on the pitch today.
transcript.cn: 助手们正在帮忙组织这件事，希望队伍不会太长 —— 今天球场上非常炎热，阳光明媚。

transcript.en: You can get food and drink at the café, and I really recommend the healthy lunch boxes for children.
transcript.cn: 你可以在咖啡馆里买到食物和饮料，我真的推荐给孩子们健康的午餐盒。

transcript.en: Also in the café, children are invited to do a football-themed drawing.
transcript.cn: 同样在咖啡馆里，孩子们被邀请画一幅足球主题的画。

transcript.en: We'll pick the best one at the end of the afternoon, so don't forget to put your name and contact details on the back.
transcript.cn: 我们会在下午结束时选出最好的一幅，所以别忘了在背面写上你的名字和联系方式。

transcript.en: That way, if you've left the stadium before then, we'll send your prize, but sadly we can't return drawings.
transcript.cn: 这样，如果你在那之前离开了体育场，我们会寄给你奖品，但遗憾的是，我们不能退回你的画。

transcript.en: I'd like to mention some features of the tour.
transcript.cn: 我想提一下这次旅行的一些特点。

transcript.en: We'll start with the three-sixty cinema experience, which has been very popular over the years, and then I'll take you to the players' dressing rooms before going outside to the seating area and the pitch.
transcript.cn: 我们将从 360 度影院体验开始，这种体验多年来一直很受欢迎。然后我会带你去球员更衣室，之后再去外面的座位区和球场。

transcript.en: I should say, if you’d prefer your visit to be self-guided, please collect headphones from the reception and then you can listen to the pre-recorded information at your own speed.
transcript.cn: 我想说，如果你更喜欢自助导览，请从接待处收集耳机，这样你就可以按照自己的节奏收听预录的信息。

transcript.en: We've only just introduced this feature, and would appreciate your feedback.
transcript.cn: 我们刚刚介绍了这个功能，希望能得到您的反馈。

transcript.en: We're thinking of offering tours in other languages in future so if you have any thoughts on that, we'd welcome those too.
transcript.cn: 我们正在考虑未来提供其他语言的巡演，所以如果你对此有任何想法，我们也欢迎。

transcript.en: If you plan to return another time, you might like to book one of our VIP tours – we've only just started offering these, and they can be booked online.
transcript.cn: 如果你打算下次再来，不妨预订我们的 VIP 旅行团 —— 我们刚刚开始提供这些服务，可以在线预订。

transcript.en: Now, the stadium you see today was built in 1989, as part of a three-year redevelopment project – while that project was going on, the team had to play its matches at the ground of another club – apart from that, the club has been here on this site since 1870.
transcript.cn: 现在，你今天看到的体育场建于 1989 年，是一个为期三年的重建项目的一部分 —— 在项目进行期间，球队不得不在另一家俱乐部的场地上进行比赛 —— 除此之外，这家俱乐部自 1870 年以来就一直在这里。

transcript.en: As some of you may know, that was the start of a really important decade in the history of football in this country.
transcript.cn: 正如你们中的一些人可能知道的，那是这个国家足球历史上一个真正重要十年的开始。

transcript.en: For example, 1870 was also the year that football teams started to include a player whose role it was to guard the goal.
transcript.cn: 例如，1870 年也是足球队开始引入一名负责守门的球员的年份。

transcript.en: It's hard to imagine what the game must have been like without someone in that position, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 很难想象如果没有人担任那个位置，比赛会是什么样子，对吧？

transcript.en: In 1872 and 73, many other clubs were established, both here and abroad, and the following year, in 1874, referees were allowed to send players off if they committed certain offences.
transcript.cn: 1872年和1873 年，国内外都建立了许多其他俱乐部。第二年，即 1874 年，如果球员犯了某些过错，裁判被允许将其解雇。

transcript.en: And also in that year, teams started having to swap ends at half-time.
transcript.cn: 也是在那一年，球队开始在中场休息时不得不交换位置。

transcript.en: One fact I was interested to discover was that in early football games, the aim was for the scorer to get the ball between two flag posts, and later between sticks joined at the top with a piece of tape.
transcript.cn: 我感兴趣的一个发现是，在早期的足球比赛中，目标是让得分手将球踢到两根旗杆之间，后来又用一段胶带将两根旗杆顶部连接起来。

transcript.en: In 1875, that tape was replaced with the solid crossbar that we're familiar with today.
transcript.cn: 1875年，那条胶带被我们今天所熟悉的实心横梁所取代。

transcript.en: 1877 saw the founding of further new clubs, and the history books tell us that in the same year, all the clubs decided to set a limit of ninety minutes for each match.
transcript.cn: 1877年，又有新的俱乐部成立，历史书告诉我们，同年，所有俱乐部都决定为每场比赛设定 90 分钟的限制。

transcript.en: Before that, it was a more casual arrangement, and this sometimes caused huge arguments – and sometimes fights – during matches, when one team called the end of the game and the other team wanted to play on to try and score a winning goal.
transcript.cn: 在此之前，这是一种更为随意的安排，这有时会在比赛中引发激烈争吵，甚至发生打斗。当一支球队宣布比赛结束，而另一支球队想要继续比赛并尝试进一个制胜球时，就会发生这种情况。

transcript.en: By 1878, the number of teams in the football league increased again.
transcript.cn: 到了 1878 年，足球联赛中的球队数量再次增加。

transcript.en: In addition, referees started using whistles, and electric lamps were installed on certain pitches.
transcript.cn: 此外，裁判开始使用哨子，某些场地还安装了电灯。

transcript.en: This was a significant change, as games could then be played in the evenings all year round.
transcript.cn: 这是一个重大变化，因为比赛可以全年在晚上进行。

transcript.en: In 1880, clubs began to charge fans for admission to games, even though players were still amateurs and had other ‘proper' jobs.
transcript.cn: 1880年，俱乐部开始向球迷收取入场费，尽管球员们仍然只是业余爱好者，还有其他 “正式” 的工作。

transcript.en: That's hard to imagine in the modern professional game where top players earn significant sums of money from both playing and commercial activities.
transcript.cn: 在现代职业游戏中，顶级玩家通过游戏和商业活动赚取可观的金钱，这是很难想象的。

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出“两名学生都感到惊讶的学习写字对儿童的两个好处”。对于选项C（提升专注力），男士用“less obvious”（不太明显）引出，暗示其意外性，女士回应“had never occurred to me before”（以前从没想过）明确表达惊讶，男士接着说“Same here”（我也是），表明两人都惊讶；对于选项E（提升空间意识），男士说“I’d never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either”（从没把空间意识和手写联系起来）表达惊讶，女士回应“I thought that too”（我也这么想的），确认两人都对此感到意外。因此C和E为正确答案。

题目要求选出“两名学生都感到惊讶的学习写字对儿童的两个好处”。对于选项C（提升专注力），男士用“less obvious”（不太明显）引出，暗示其意外性，女士回应“had never occurred to me before”（以前从没想过）明确表达惊讶，男士接着说“Same here”（我也是），表明两人都惊讶；对于选项E（提升空间意识），男士说“I’d never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either”（从没把空间意识和手写联系起来）表达惊讶，女士回应“I thought that too”（我也这么想的），确认两人都对此感到意外。因此C和E为正确答案。

questions.title: What does the woman say about using laptops to teach writing to children with dyslexia?

options.title: A
options.content: Children often lack motivation to learn that way.

options.title: C
options.content: Children react more positively if they make a mistake.

questions.analysis: 原文中女士提到“如果他们搞错了，（用键盘打字）对他们来说就没那么令人沮丧——在键盘上，他们会更愿意去试一试”。其中“not as frustrating”（没那么沮丧）和“more willing to have a go”（更愿意尝试）共同表达了孩子们犯错时的积极反应，这与选项C“Children react more positively if they make a mistake”（如果犯了错，孩子们的反应会更积极）是同义替换，因此C为正确答案。

questions.title: When discussing whether to teach cursive or print writing, the woman thinks that

options.title: B

questions.analysis: 题目询问讨论教草书还是印刷体时女士的观点。根据原文，女士明确提到“cursive is more difficult to learn – especially for children with learning difficulties”（草书更难学——尤其是对有学习困难的孩子），即草书对特定群体（有学习困难的孩子）不利，这与选项A“cursive writing disadvantages a certain group of children”（草书对某一特定群体的孩子不利）表述一致，因此A为正确答案。

questions.title: According to the students, what impact does poor handwriting have on exam performance?

options.title: A

options.title: C

questions.analysis: 题目询问“差的书写对考试表现有什么影响”。原文中女士明确提到“Marks are definitely affected if examiners can’t read the script”（如果考官看不懂卷面，分数肯定会受影响），其中“Marks”（分数）与选项A中的“grades”（成绩）为同义替换，“affected”（受影响）直接对应，因此选项A正确。

questions.title: What prediction does the man make about the future of handwriting?

options.title: A
options.content: Touch typing will be taught before writing by hand.

options.title: B
options.content: Children will continue to learn to write by hand.

options.title: C
options.content: People will dislike handwriting on digital devices.

questions.analysis: 男士在回答关于手写未来的问题时提到“我不认为那（手写的作用）会有很大改变”，并补充“老师们明白手写的价值，它是一项基本生活技能”。这表明手写的重要性不会发生显著变化，因此孩子们会继续学习手写，与选项B“Children will continue to learn to write by hand”的含义一致。

questions.title: The woman is concerned that relying on digital devices has made it difficult for her to
questions.analysis: 文中女士明确提到她的妈妈对她糟糕的拼写（awful spelling）和不规范的标点（punctuation is really inconsistent）感到震惊，并且她认为这是由于缺乏练习（lack of practice）。结合前文提到的人们越来越少手写，更多依赖数码设备（relying on digital devices），可以推断出女士担忧的是依赖数码设备导致她在拼写和标点方面遇到困难。因此，正确答案是B。

questions.title: How do the students feel about their own handwriting?

options.title: A
options.content: concerned they are unable to write quickly

options.title: B
options.content: embarrassed by comments made about it

options.title: C
options.content: regretful that they have lost the habit

questions.analysis: 文中男士提到"Personally, I miss writing by hand. I hardly ever write anything now... It’s a shame"（就我个人而言，我很怀念手写。我现在几乎不写任何东西了……真遗憾），女士回应"I feel the same way. I used to write a diary by hand and now I do that digitally"（我也有同感。我过去常常用手写日记，现在都用电子方式写了）。这些表述清晰地体现了他们对自己已经失去手写习惯这件事感到遗憾，与选项C "regretful that they have lost the habit"（遗憾他们已经失去了这个习惯）的含义完全相符。

transcript.en: PART 3
MAN: How are you getting on with the assignment on handwriting?
transcript.cn: 男人：你手写作业做得怎么样了？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Not too bad. You know, I hadn't realised that children benefit in so many ways from learning to write.
transcript.cn: 女人：还不错。你知道吗，我之前没有意识到，孩子们从学习写字中获益如此之多。

transcript.en: It's such an important skill and yet most people think handwriting is less important than in the past because people hardly ever write by hand these days.
transcript.cn: 这是一项如此重要的技能，然而大多数人认为手写不如过去重要，因为如今人们几乎不再用手写字了。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes. And all the evidence suggests children should learn to write by hand before they learn to type. Not least because it helps their memory.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的。所有证据都表明，孩子们应该在学习打字之前先学习手写。尤其是因为这有助于他们的记忆力。

transcript.en: WOMAN: That's right. The physical act of writing helps children to remember letters.
transcript.cn: 女人：没错。书写这一身体行为有助于孩子们记住字母。

transcript.en: That seems pretty obvious when you think about it.
transcript.cn: 仔细想想，这似乎是相当明显的。

transcript.en: MAN: What's less obvious is how it helps develop their concentration.
transcript.cn: 男人：不那么明显的是它如何帮助培养他们的专注力。

transcript.en: They have to sit still and focus on one thing.
transcript.cn: 他们必须静静地坐着，专注于一件事。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yeah. That aspect of handwriting had never occurred to me before.
transcript.cn: 女人：是的，我以前从未想过笔迹的这个方面。

transcript.en: MAN: Same here. I'm not sure I understand how it improves children's imagination though.
transcript.cn: 男人：这里也一样。不过，我不确定我是否理解它如何提高孩子们的想象力。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Well, there was that study which showed that primary aged children generated more ideas when they were writing by hand than using a keyboard. I would've guessed that would be the case.
transcript.cn: 女：嗯，有一项研究表明，初级儿童在手写时产生的想法比使用键盘时更多。我早就猜到会是这样。

transcript.en: MAN: Mmm, yeah. I'd never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either.
transcript.cn: 男人：嗯，是的。我也从未将空间意识与手写联系起来。

transcript.en: I thought spatial awareness was more to do with knowing where you are in relation to objects or other people.
transcript.cn: 我认为空间意识更多地与知道自己与物体或其他人的相对位置有关。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I thought that too. But good spatial awareness is essential for writing because you have to space words correctly.
transcript.cn: 女人：我也这么认为。但是，良好的空间意识对写作至关重要，因为你必须正确地给词语留出空间。

transcript.en: It's not just fine motor skills that improve through writing as I'd always assumed.
transcript.cn: 不仅仅是像我一直以为的那样，通过写作可以提高优秀的运动技能。

transcript.en: MAN: Handwriting is so much harder for children with dyspraxia, who have problems coordinating movement.
transcript.cn: 男人：对于有运动障碍的儿童来说，手写要困难得多，他们在协调运动方面存在问题。

transcript.en: It's good there are lots of things you can do in the classroom to help them.
transcript.cn: 好在你可以在课堂上做很多事情来帮助他们。

transcript.en: They need so much more support with letter formation – you need to play lots of games to help them distinguish letter shapes. It takes a lot of patience.
transcript.cn: 他们在字母形成方面需要更多的支持 —— 你需要玩很多游戏来帮助他们区分字母的形状。这需要很大的耐心。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yeah. I liked the idea of using one of those pens that lights up if you press too hard.
transcript.cn: 女人：是的。我喜欢使用那种用力过猛就会发光的笔的想法。

transcript.en: That seems like a really simple solution.
transcript.cn: 这似乎是一个非常简单的解决方案。

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, absolutely. I'm not sure there's much you can do about children with dyspraxia writing very slowly.
transcript.cn: 男人：是的，绝对如此。我不确定对于写作障碍儿童写得很慢的问题，你能做些什么。

transcript.en: It's more important to focus on accuracy and as they get more confident I think they eventually speed up.
transcript.cn: 更重要的是关注准确性，随着他们变得越来越自信，我认为他们最终会加快速度。

transcript.en: WOMAN: One quite simple thing you can do is to use grid paper.
transcript.cn: 女人：有一件相当简单的事情你可以做，那就是使用方格纸。

transcript.en: So they write each letter in a box and that trains them to space the letters correctly.
transcript.cn: 所以他们把每个字母都写在一个方框里，这样就能训练他们正确地排列字母。

transcript.en: MAN: Indeed, that's more important for legibility than trying to get them to write in a straight line.
transcript.cn: 男人：确实，这对于提高可读性比试图让他们写直线更重要。

transcript.en: MAN: For some children it might be better to teach them to write on a laptop rather than by hand.
transcript.cn: 男人：对于一些孩子来说，教他们用笔记本电脑写字可能比用手写更好。

transcript.en: Like children with dyslexia. They often really struggle with handwriting and some just give up.
transcript.cn: 就像患有阅读障碍的孩子一样。他们经常在书写方面遇到困难，有些人干脆就放弃了。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yeah. It's not as frustrating for them if they get things wrong – on a keyboard they can be more willing to have a go.
transcript.cn: 女人：是的。对他们来说，即使写错了东西也不会那么沮丧 —— 在键盘上，他们可能会更愿意尝试。

transcript.en: But I read that developing fluency isn't any faster.
transcript.cn: 但我读到，培养流畅性的速度并没有更快。

transcript.en: MAN: That's right. Did you read that article on the benefits of teaching print rather than cursive handwriting, where the letters are joined up?
transcript.cn: 男人：没错。你有没有读过那篇关于教授印刷体而不是连字的草体书法的文章？

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes. Well in the past cursive writing was certainly considered more stylish and educated.
transcript.cn: 女人：是的。在过去，草体字确实被认为更有风格，也更有教育意义。

transcript.en: But not anymore. Teachers' attitudes have changed because it's been proved that cursive is more difficult to learn – especially for children with learning difficulties who find joining up letters really challenging.
transcript.cn: 但现在不同了。教师的态度已经改变，因为事实证明，草书更难学习 —— 特别是对于有学习障碍的孩子来说，他们发现连接字母真的很有挑战性。

transcript.en: MAN: I agree. I was always worried that my poor handwriting affected my exam results.
transcript.cn: 男人：我同意。我一直担心我糟糕的书法会影响我的考试成绩。

transcript.en: And now research shows that I was right to worry.
transcript.cn: 现在研究表明，我的担忧是正确的。

transcript.en: I'm sure a lot of students think it's unfair that they're being judged on their handwriting not just their knowledge.
transcript.cn: 我相信许多学生都认为，不仅仅根据他们的知识来评判他们，还要根据他们的笔迹来评判他们，这是不公平的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Marks are definitely affected if examiners can't read the script.
transcript.cn: 女人：如果考官不能阅读笔迹，成绩肯定会受到影响。

transcript.en: That's why it's always been so important to teach children to write legibly…
transcript.cn: 这就是为什么教孩子们写出清晰易读的字一直如此重要......

transcript.en: Do you think the role of handwriting will change in the future?
transcript.cn: 你认为书法在未来的作用会改变吗？

transcript.en: MAN: I can't see that changing much.
transcript.cn: 男人：我看不出有什么变化。

transcript.en: Touch typing still isn't taught in most schools, which is a shame.
transcript.cn: 大多数学校仍然没有教授触摸式打字，这是一个遗憾。

transcript.en: But maybe that won't be necessary in the future because people will also be able to write by hand on digital devices. Anyway, teachers understand the value of handwriting. It's a basic life skill.
transcript.cn: 但也许在未来这将不再是必要的，因为人们也将能够在数字设备上手写。无论如何，老师们理解手写的价值。这是一项基本的生活技能。

transcript.en: WOMAN: True. However, the fact is that people are writing by hand less and less and relying on digital devices. That does cause some problems.
transcript.cn: 女人：没错。然而，事实是人们越来越少用手写，越来越依赖数字设备。这确实造成了一些问题。

transcript.en: MAN: You mean like note taking? There are lots of apps for that.
transcript.cn: 男人：你是说像做笔记那样的吗？有很多这样的应用程序。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And for reading historical documents apparently.
transcript.cn: 女人：显然还有阅读历史文献的应用。

transcript.en: But my mum is shocked by my awful spelling and the fact that my punctuation is really inconsistent.
transcript.cn: 但我妈妈对我糟糕的拼写和标点符号的不一致感到震惊。

transcript.en: I think you can put that down to lack of practice.
transcript.cn: 我想你可以把这归因于缺乏练习。

transcript.en: MAN: I expect so. Personally, I miss writing by hand. I hardly ever write anything now.
transcript.cn: 男人：我想是的。就我个人而言，我怀念手写。现在我几乎不写任何东西了。

transcript.en: I remember my grandparents had such beautiful handwriting and it was so individual.
transcript.cn: 我记得我的祖父母有着非常优美的字迹，而且非常独特。

transcript.en: Nobody I know would be able to identify my handwriting now. It's a shame.
transcript.cn: 现在我认识的人没有一个能认出我的笔迹了。真遗憾。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I know. I feel the same way.
transcript.cn: 女人：我知道，我也有同感。

transcript.en: I used to write a diary by hand and now I do that digitally.
transcript.cn: 我以前用手写日记，现在我用数字化的方式写。

transcript.en: It just seems less effort to do it that way. So it's not just a problem…
transcript.cn: 只是用那种方式似乎更省力。所以这不仅仅是个问题......

groups.title: Research in the area around the Chembe Bird Sanctuary
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: The importance of birds of prey to the local communities · They destroy $22105$ and other rodents. · They help to prevent farmers from being bitten by $22106$ · They have been an important part of the local culture for many years. · They now support the economy by encouraging $22107$ in the area. Falling numbers of birds of prey · The birds may be accidentally killed - by $22108$ when they are hunting or sleeping - by electrocution from contact with power lines, especially at times when there is a lot of $22109$ · Local farmers may illegally shoot them or $22110$ them. Ways of protecting chickens from birds of prey · clearing away vegetation from the area (unhelpful) · providing a $22111$ for chickens (expensive) · frightening birds of prey by - keeping a $22112$ - making a $22113$ - e.g. with metal objects · A $22114$ of methods is usually most effective.

questions.analysis: 题目要求填入“它们（鸟类）消灭【 】以及其他啮齿动物”，需要找一种具体的啮齿动物。首先，题干中的“rodents”（啮齿动物）是核心定位词，在原文中直接出现；“destroy”（消灭）的同义替换是原文中的“hunted and killed”（捕食）。答案句明确提到“rodents such as rats”（像老鼠这样的啮齿动物），“such as”是举例信号词，说明“rats”（老鼠）是“rodents”的具体例子，符合题干“【 】 and other rodents”的并列结构，所以答案是“rats”。

这道题问的是“它们（猛禽）帮助防止农民被什么咬伤”。首先，题干中的定位词“farmers”在原文中被同义替换为“rural workers”（农村劳动者，即农民），“bitten”被同义替换为名词“bite”（咬伤）。原文明确提到“对农村劳动者来说，一个主要的危险是蛇，蛇的咬伤可能是危险的，甚至是致命的”，而猛禽的作用是“控制蛇的数量”，这意味着猛禽能帮助农民避免被蛇咬伤。因此，答案是“snakes”。

这道题问的是“它们现在通过鼓励该地区的什么来支持经济”。首先，我们用题干中的“support the economy”作为定位词，在原文中找到了它的同义替换“in economic terms”和“source of revenue”。同时，题干中的“now”在原文中被替换成了“more recently”和“at present”。当听到这些信号词时，我们就知道答案区域到了。原文接着明确指出“tourism has become an important source of revenue”，也就是说旅游业成为了重要的收入来源，这正好回答了“通过鼓励什么来支持经济”的问题。所以，正确答案就是“tourism”。

这道题的答案是“traffic”。首先，题干中的定位词是“hunting”和“sleeping”。在听力原文中，“hunting”的同义替换是“catch and eat their prey”，“sleeping”的同义替换是“sleep”。当听到“catch and eat their prey”时，对应题干的“hunting”场景，原文明确提到“are hit by fast-moving traffic”，说明鸟类捕猎时会被车辆撞到；接着提到“birds may regard the quieter roads as safe places to sleep”，对应题干的“sleeping”场景，进一步说明鸟类睡觉时也在道路上，会面临被车辆伤害的风险。因此，“traffic”是导致鸟类在捕猎或睡觉时死亡的直接原因，所以答案是“traffic”。

同学你看，这道题问的是“by electrocution from contact with power lines, especially at times when there is a lot of 【 】”。首先，我们要抓住题干里的定位词“electrocution”和“power lines”。在听力原文中，你会听到“Accidental deaths may also occur if these birds fly close to high-voltage power lines, as they may be electrocuted.”这里的“electrocuted”就是“electrocution”的同义替换，“high-voltage power lines”是“power lines”的同义替换，这就告诉我们答案就在附近了。紧接着，原文说“This is a particular danger in the heavy rain...”，这里的“particular danger”对应题干的“especially”，“heavy rain”（大雨）就对应了“a lot of rain”。题目要求只填一个词，所以答案就是“rain”。因为原文明确指出在有大量雨（heavy rain）的时候，接触电线导致触电的危险特别大，所以“rain”是正确答案。

这道题的题干结构是“Local farmers may illegally shoot them or 【 】 them”，需要填入一个与“shoot”并列的动词。首先，通过定位词“Local farmers”和“shoot”，我们在原文中找到关键句：“farmers may shoot these birds... or they may poison the birds”。这里“shoot”和“poison”通过“or”构成并列关系，与题干中“shoot them or... them”的结构完全对应。同时，题干中的“illegally”也与原文“which is illegal”“which again is illegal”呼应，进一步确认“poison”是正确答案。所以空格处应填入“poison”。

题目问的是“providing a 【】 for chickens (expensive)”，即需要找到一个为鸡提供的、且昂贵的东西。在听力原文中，当讨论如何保护鸡免受猛禽威胁时，提到了“Another possibility would be to prevent the chickens from going outside at all, and to keep them safe from predators inside a building...”，这里明确指出了“inside a building”，即把鸡放在建筑物里面。紧接着又提到“but this would cost far too much to be a practical solution”，“cost far too much”对应了题目中的“expensive”。因此，为鸡提供的这个昂贵的东西就是“building”，所以答案是building。

这道题的题干是“keeping a 【 】”，这里的核心定位词是“keeping”，它表示“饲养、拥有”的意思。在听力原文中，我们需要找到与“keeping”意思相近的词来定位答案。原文提到“Most of the farmers had at least one dog...”，这里的“had”就是“keeping”的同义替换，意思是“拥有”。紧接着这句话就明确指出了农民拥有的是“dog”，并且说明了狗能帮助吓走捕食者，这与题干中“keeping a...”的语境完全相符，所以答案自然就是“dog”。

同学你看，题目问的是“making a 【 】 - e.g. with metal objects”，也就是要用金属物品制造出什么。这里的定位词是“metal objects”。在听力原文中，当提到农民如何驱赶猛禽时，出现了“hit pans with a metal spoon”，这里的“pans”（平底锅）和“metal spoon”（金属勺子）就是“metal objects”的具体例子，也就是信号词。紧接着，原文说“so that the resulting noise would succeed in driving away birds”，意思是“以便由此产生的噪音能成功赶走鸟”。题目中的“making a...”正是对应这里的“resulting”（产生的），所以制造出的东西就是“noise”。因此，答案是“noise”。

同学你看，这道题问的是“A ______ of methods is usually most effective.”。首先，我们在题干里找定位词，“methods”（方法）和“effective”（有效的）就是关键。在听力原文中，当提到“None of these methods was a hundred per cent effective”时，这里的“methods”和“effective”就对应上了题干的定位词，这是一个重要的信号。接着，原文用“so as a result”（所以结果是）进行总结，说村民们“被迫使用多种方法的组合（a combination）才能有一点效果（to have any effect）”。这里的“have any effect”其实就是题干中“effective”的意思。题目问的是“什么样的方法通常最有效”，原文明确指出不是只用一种方法，而是需要“combination”（组合），所以“combination”就是正确答案啦。

transcript.en: PART 4
We've been looking at different types of conflicts that may arise between wildlife and humans at the boundaries of protected areas such as national parks and animal sanctuaries.
transcript.cn: 我们一直在研究野生动物和人类在国家公园和动物保护区等保护区域边界可能发生的不同类型的冲突。

transcript.en: I'd like to illustrate this by telling you about some research that I've been involved in recently in the central African country of Zambia in the area around the Chembe Bird Sanctuary, which contains over three hundred of the listed birds of Zambia.
transcript.cn: 为了说明这一点，我想告诉你我最近在非洲中部国家赞比亚进行的一些研究，这些研究位于钦贝鸟类保护区周边地区，该保护区包含了赞比亚列出的 300 多种鸟类。

transcript.en: These include a number of birds of prey, such as eagles, hawks and owls, that live by hunting and killing other birds and animals.
transcript.cn: 其中包括一些捕食性鸟类，如鹰、隼和猫头鹰，它们以猎杀其他鸟类和动物为生。

transcript.en: Now, most of the people living in the local communities near to the bird sanctuary are small-scale farmers, and these birds of prey provide important social and ecological benefits to them.
transcript.cn: 如今，生活在鸟类保护区附近当地社区的大多数人都是小规模农民，这些猛禽为他们提供了重要的社会和生态利益。

transcript.en: For example, a lot of damage can be caused to farmers' crops by rodents such as rats, which would consume the crops as they grow in the fields as well as after harvesting if they weren't hunted and killed by the birds.
transcript.cn: 例如，老鼠等啮齿类动物可能会对农民的作物造成很大的破坏。如果这些动物没有被鸟类捕杀，它们就会在作物生长期间和收割后吃掉它们。

transcript.en: And the predatory habits of these birds also protect farmers in other ways.
transcript.cn: 这些鸟类的捕食习性也在其他方面保护了农民。

transcript.en: For example, a major danger to rural workers is snakes, whose bite may be dangerous or even fatal, and birds of prey have a major role in keeping their populations under control.
transcript.cn: 例如，对农村工人来说，蛇是一种主要危险，蛇的咬伤可能是危险的，甚至是致命的，而猛禽在控制其数量方面发挥着重要作用。

transcript.en: Local people have always been aware of these benefits and for years, even before the sanctuary was opened in 1973, the birds played a key role in the culture of the region.
transcript.cn: 当地人一直意识到这些好处，多年来，甚至在 1973 年保护区开放之前，这些鸟类就在该地区的文化中发挥着关键作用。

transcript.en: However, more recently, the sanctuary and its birds have also become increasingly important to the community in economic terms, because at present, after a relatively slow start, tourism has become an important source of revenue for them.
transcript.cn: 然而，近年来，这个保护区及其鸟类在经济方面对社区的重要性也越来越大，因为目前，在经历了相对缓慢的起步之后，旅游业已经成为它们重要的收入来源。

transcript.en: However, although these birds of prey are protected by the government, their numbers are falling.
transcript.cn: 然而，尽管这些猛禽受到政府的保护，但它们的数量正在下降。

transcript.en: Some of these deaths are accidental. Fatalities occur when birds alight on roads to catch and eat their prey, and are hit by fast-moving traffic.
transcript.cn: 其中一些死亡是意外事故。死亡事件发生在鸟类降落在道路上捕食猎物时，以及被快速行驶的车辆撞击时。

transcript.en: Drivers in Zambia have to take special care at night, as birds may regard the quieter roads as safe places to sleep.
transcript.cn: 赞比亚的司机在夜间必须特别小心，因为鸟类可能会将更安静的道路视为安全的栖息地。

transcript.en: Accidental deaths may also occur if these birds fly close to high-voltage power lines, as they may be electrocuted.
transcript.cn: 如果这些鸟类飞近高压电线，也可能发生意外死亡，因为它们可能会触电身亡。

transcript.en: This is a particular danger in the heavy rain which can occur in the region in the months from December to April.
transcript.cn: 这是该地区在 12 月至 4 月期间可能发生的大雨中的一种特殊危险。

transcript.en: And local farmers also pose a threat to these birds.
transcript.cn: 当地农民也对这些鸟类构成威胁。

transcript.en: As well as growing crops, small-scale farmers in the area also rear chickens.
transcript.cn: 除了种植农作物，该地区的小规模农民还养鸡。

transcript.en: These provide food for the farmers' families, as well as being an important source of income.
transcript.cn: 这些鸟类为农民家庭提供食物，同时也是重要的收入来源。

transcript.en: But they're also an easy target for birds of prey, and so farmers may shoot these birds, which is illegal but understandable, or they may poison the birds, which again is illegal and can have negative effects on the ecosystem.
transcript.cn: 但它们也是猛禽的容易猎杀目标，因此农民可能会射杀这些鸟类，这虽然违法，但可以理解。或者他们可能会毒杀这些鸟类，这同样是非法的，可能会对生态系统产生负面影响。

transcript.en: So how else can farmers protect their chickens from birds of prey?
transcript.cn: 那么，农民还能如何保护他们的鸡免受猛禽的攻击呢？

transcript.en: Some people believe that to prevent the predators from settling near the area where the chickens are kept it's best to keep this area free from vegetation.
transcript.cn: 一些人认为，为了防止捕食者在养鸡区域附近定居，最好保持该区域不受植被影响。

transcript.en: But in fact this is counter-productive as it means the chickens have no cover to hide in and they'll be easier for the birds to see.
transcript.cn: 但事实上，这种做法适得其反，因为这意味着鸡没有遮蔽物可以躲藏，而且它们更容易被鸟类发现。

transcript.en: Another possibility would be to prevent the chickens from going outside at all, and to keep them safe from predators inside a building, but this would cost far too much to be a practical solution.
transcript.cn: 另一种可能性是完全禁止鸡外出，并保护它们免受建筑物内捕食者的伤害，但这将花费太多，无法成为一个切实可行的解决方案。

transcript.en: Nearly all the farmers reported that they spent a lot of time and effort trying to frighten off the birds of prey without actually harming them.
transcript.cn: 几乎所有农民都报告说，他们花费了大量时间和精力试图在不真正伤害猎物的情况下吓跑它们。

transcript.en: Most of the farmers had at least one dog, and said this was a big help at scaring away the predators.
transcript.cn: 大多数农民至少养一条狗，他们说这对吓跑捕食者有很大帮助。

transcript.en: Some of the farmers also reported that during the breeding season, when the chickens were particularly vulnerable, they encouraged their children to watch over the chickens and to hit pans with a metal spoon so that the resulting noise would succeed in driving away birds that were trying to seize the young chicks.
transcript.cn: 一些农民还报告说，在繁殖季节，当小鸡特别脆弱时，他们鼓励孩子们照看小鸡，并用金属勺子敲打平底锅，这样产生的噪音就能成功驱赶那些试图抓住小鸡的鸟类。

transcript.en: None of these methods was a hundred per cent effective, so as a result, the village people told us that rather than just using one method, they were forced to use a combination for them to have any effect.
transcript.cn: 这些方法都不是百分之百有效的，因此，村民告诉我们，他们不是只使用一种方法，而是被迫使用多种方法组合才能产生效果。

transcript.en: And even so, these birds of prey remain a major threat to the chickens' survival and cause considerable economic loss to farmers.
transcript.cn: 尽管如此，这些捕食性鸟类仍然是鸡生存的主要威胁，并给农民造成了相当大的经济损失。

transcript.en: So we looked at the possibility of a longer-term solution to ...
transcript.cn: 因此，我们开始考虑找到一个长期解决方案的可能性......
